summary: Frontend-only testing of new Pricing page (/pricing) and SEO metadata across all 6 routes. All 14 testable items passed: pricing page renders, 3 tier cards (Construction/Framework/Infrastructure), feature counts match spec (6/8/8), prices correct, RECOMMENDED badge on Framework tier, all CTAs navigate correctly (Start Building->/auth, Select Framework->/auth, Contact Sales->/contact), 4 FAQs render, Enterprise CTA links to /contact, navbar shows 5 links (Platform, Developers, Security, Pricing, Contact), Pricing nav link is highlighted (text-[#149d80]) on /pricing, and unique <title>/meta description/og:title/og:description tags are set on every one of the 6 pages.
critical_code_review_comments: SEOHead.jsx is a client-side meta updater (useEffect). It works for runtime UX and crawlers that execute JS, but bots without JS execution will only see the static index.html title. For real SEO this should ideally be SSR or react-helmet-async with a prerender; not blocking for an MVP marketing site.

PricingPage.jsx is ~325 lines and starts to mix data (tiers/faqs arrays) with markup. Consider extracting tiers and faqs into separate data modules if more pages reuse them, but acceptable for current size.
No canonical <link rel=canonical> or twitter:card meta tags emitted by SEOHead - minor SEO gap worth adding.
test_credentials: N/A - no auth testing in this iteration
context_for_next_testing_agent: All previous flows (iteration_1 covered home/onboarding/security/auth/contact) and new Pricing + SEOHead are verified. Frontend URL used: https://girderpay-vault.preview.emergentagent.com (from frontend/.env). Pricing data-testids present: pricing-page, pricing-tier-0/1/2, pricing-cta-0/1/2, pricing-faq-0/1/2/3, pricing-recommended-badge, pricing-badge, pricing-enterprise-cta. Navbar nav-link-{platform|developers|security|pricing|contact} confirmed.
